key_themes: Explosive AI semiconductor and custom ASIC growth (106% YoY in Q1 to $8.4B)
Expanding long-term hyperscaler partnerships (Google to 2031, Meta to 2029, Anthropic expansions)
Strong Q1 earnings beat, raised Q2 guidance ($22B revenue), and new $10B share repurchase
Bullish analyst upgrades with price targets $435–$525 and hypergrowth projections
Deep moat in AI networking, custom chips reducing hyperscaler Nvidia dependency
summary: Broadcom (AVGO) is seeing strongly bullish sentiment driven by record Q1 FY2026 results, with revenue up 29% to $19.3B and AI revenue surging 106% to $8.4B on custom accelerators and networking demand. The company raised Q2 guidance to ~$22B revenue with $10.7B in AI semis, authorized a $10B buyback, and expanded multi-year deals with Google (through 2031), Meta, and Anthropic that add tens of billions in visibility and support $100B+ annual AI revenue ambitions by 2027. Investors on X and analysts view it as a core AI infrastructure compounder with durable margins and pricing power, fueling recent stock strength near $400–$406 with consensus Buy ratings and further upside projected.
sources: 15+ recent news articles (Q1 FY2026 earnings, analyst notes from UBS/Bernstein/Goldman, AI partnership announcements), 25+ investor X posts (predominantly bullish discussions on AI backlog, price targets, and conviction holds)
